Name: curvature_aware_precision_routing
Title: Curvature-Aware Precision Routing: Training-Free Inference via Deterministic Spectral Estimators
Short Hypothesis: Precision allocation in neural networks can be optimized using deterministic spectral estimators derived from activation geometry, ensuring numerical stability and computational efficiency without training.
Related Work: Existing methods like Spectral Quantization Routing (SQR) focus on activation geometry but lack explicit numerical stability analysis. Our proposal integrates PAC-Bayes generalization bounds and deterministic spectral estimators, providing a more rigorous and transparent framework.
Abstract: We propose Curvature-Aware Precision Routing (CAPR), a training-free framework for optimizing precision allocation in neural networks. CAPR leverages deterministic spectral estimators derived from activation geometry to allocate numerical precision dynamically. By integrating PAC-Bayes generalization bounds and explicit numerical stability analysis, CAPR ensures computational efficiency and robustness under precision switching. We demonstrate the effectiveness of CAPR on single-GPU setups across various benchmarks, achieving superior throughput-accuracy Pareto frontiers compared to existing methods. Our approach bridges foundational optimization theory with practical deployment constraints, offering a mathematically transparent and resource-efficient solution for precision routing.
Experiments: Evaluate CAPR on single-GPU setups across WILDS benchmarks.
Compare CAPR with static and heuristic mixed-precision baselines.
Measure throughput, accuracy, and numerical stability using spectral diagnostics.
Validate PAC-Bayes generalization bounds under quantization noise.
Risk Factors and Limitations: Potential overhead from deterministic spectral estimators.
Limited applicability to architectures with highly irregular activation geometries.
Dependence on accurate PAC-Bayes bounds for generalization stability.
